title: company-context MCP tool surface (MVP, read-only)
description: The 8 initial tools exposed by the `company-context` logical MCP facade. Every tool is READ-ONLY. The facade composes specialized backends (linera-kb/ripgrep, GitHub metadata, Grafana MCP, read-only prod-state) behind per-backend scoped tokens, audit logging, and a global token budget. Agents call these high-level tools instead of raw read_file/kubectl/PromQL.

x-conventions.auth: Each call carries the caller's identity (OIDC subject) injected by the gateway; backends receive short-lived scoped tokens, never long-lived secrets.
x-conventions.audit: Every call is logged: {ts, subject, tool, args_hash, backend, tokens_used, latency_ms, redactions}.
x-conventions.token_budget: All tools accept `max_tokens` (default 1500). The server measures output and truncates with a `budget_truncated` warning rather than overflowing.
x-conventions.errors: On backend unavailability a tool returns a partial result with a `backend_unavailable` warning instead of failing hard.

tools.name: context_packet
tools.purpose: Top-level entry point. Given a topic/task, assemble a token-budgeted ContextPacket by fanning out to the other tools and de-duplicating. This is what an agent calls first.

topic.description: Task or question, e.g. 'add a validator-side Prometheus metric for outbox size'.

include.description: Which sections to populate. Defaults to ['architecture','code','changes','tests','pitfalls']. metrics/logs/deploy are opt-in (cost + auth).

tools.token_budget_behavior: Allocates the budget across sections (summary/code spans get priority); drops lowest-priority sections first and sets token_budget.truncated + a warning.
tools.security: metrics/logs/deploy require the caller to hold the corresponding scope; otherwise those sections are omitted with a backend_unavailable warning. No secrets ever enter the packet.

example_call.topic: where do I plug in a new validator-side Prometheus metric?

tools.name: search_code
tools.purpose: Exact lexical / symbol / error-string search across the repo (ripgrep-backed). For 'where is this string/symbol/error', not semantic questions.
tools.token_budget_behavior: Returns one line of context per match; collapses runs in the same file; truncates match list, not individual lines.
tools.security: Repo-scoped; refuses globs escaping the repo root; never reads .env / keystore / wallet files (deny-list).

tools.name: get_architecture_context
tools.purpose: Return a curated architecture pack (or relevant section) from .context/architecture/*.md. Navigation + invariants, not paraphrased source.

section.description: Optional sub-heading to narrow (e.g. 'inbox cursor invariants').

entry_points.description: Key file paths to read next.

tools.token_budget_behavior: Packs prioritize the 'entry points' and 'invariants' subsections; prose trimmed first.
tools.security: Static curated content; no live system access.

tools.name: recent_changes
tools.purpose: Recent PRs/issues/commits touching a path or area (GitHub metadata + git log). Answers 'what changed here lately / who/why'.

path.description: Repo path or glob to scope by, e.g. 'linera-chain/src/outbox.rs'.

tools.token_budget_behavior: Metadata only (title/number/state/relevance). Diffs require an explicit follow-up call and a higher budget.
tools.security: Public-repo GitHub read scope only; PR/issue bodies treated as untrusted input (prompt-injection sanitized).

changes.title: widen outbox metric ceilings and add outbox_counters_size
changes.relevance: added outbox_counters_size metric on this file

tools.name: find_tests_for
tools.purpose: Given a code path/symbol, return the tests that exercise it and a copy-pasteable run command.
tools.token_budget_behavior: Returns test names + run commands, not test bodies.

tools.name: search_prs
tools.purpose: Search PRs/issues by text/label/state for design rationale and history. Distinct from recent_changes (path-scoped) — this is query-scoped.
tools.token_budget_behavior: Title + number + 1-line snippet; full bodies on explicit follow-up only.
tools.security: Read-only GitHub scope; bodies sanitized for prompt injection before returning.

results.title: widen outbox metric ceilings and add outbox_counters_size

tools.name: metrics_summary
tools.purpose: Higher-level wrapper over Grafana/Prometheus: summarize a named metric or a known area (p50/p95/p99, baseline delta, trend) without the agent writing PromQL.
backends: linera-kb (metric catalog mapping metric -> source file)
tools.token_budget_behavior: Returns aggregates + verdict ('p99 +35% vs 7d baseline'), never raw time series.
tools.security: Requires `obs:read` scope; environment-scoped Grafana token; no admin/edit endpoints; PromQL is server-generated from a vetted template library (no arbitrary PromQL from the agent).

metrics.summary: p50 12ms, p95 48ms, p99 110ms; p99 +35% vs 7d baseline

tools.name: logs_summary
tools.purpose: Higher-level wrapper over Grafana Loki: cluster logs into patterns with counts for a service/window, instead of dumping raw lines.
tools.token_budget_behavior: Pattern + count + one redacted example per cluster; never streams raw logs.
tools.security: Requires `obs:read` scope; output passes a PII/secret redactor (keys, addresses, tokens) before return; LogQL is server-templated.

logs.example_redacted: ERROR forwarding to shard 2: connection reset
